hero.title: Modal Dialog
hero.subtitle: Open and close modals, and discover the design intent behind each step.

basic.title: Controlled vs Uncontrolled
basic.description: Two ways to open a modal. Controlled mode lets the parent manage the isOpen state directly, while uncontrolled mode toggles its own state on Trigger click.
basic.hint: Click both buttons to open each modal
basic.insight: Use controlled mode when external events (API responses, timers) need to open the modal. Use uncontrolled mode for simple button toggles.
basic.controlledBody: The parent component manages isOpen and onClose directly.
basic.uncontrolledBody: Click the Trigger to open, and the modal manages its own state.

composition.title: Why compound components?
composition.description: Compare a single component with all props vs. a compound component split by role.
composition.insight: The compound pattern allows free customization per slot while sharing internal state via Context.
composition.body: Freely compose Header, Body, and Footer as you like.

portal.title: Portal rendering
portal.description: What happens when you open a modal inside an overflow: hidden container? Without Portal, the modal gets clipped.
portal.hint: Click both buttons to compare the difference with and without Portal
portal.insight: Floating UI elements (modals, tooltips, dropdowns) must escape the DOM hierarchy. React Portal solves this.
portal.clippedNote: Without Portal, the modal gets clipped inside this area.
portal.body: Can you see this modal without clipping?

focusTrap.title: Focus trap
focusTrap.description: When the modal opens, focus is trapped inside. Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through elements without escaping.
focusTrap.hint: Open the modal and try Tab to cycle focus
focusTrap.insight: Focus trap in 4 steps — save previous focus → move to first element → Tab loop → restore on close. Essential for screen reader accessibility.
focusTrap.name: Name

animation.title: Animation separation
animation.description: Overlay (fade-in) and content (slide-up) have independent animations. In a parent-child structure, opacity propagates and breaks things.
animation.hint: Toggle between the two structures to compare animations
animation.insight: When overlay and content are parent-child, the parent's opacity propagates to the child, breaking the animation. Sibling separation is key.
animation.body: Check if overlay fade and content slide work independently.

nativeDialog.title: Native <dialog> comparison
nativeDialog.description: Compare HTML5 <dialog> tag with our custom modal side by side. Try Tab to see focus escape in the native dialog.
nativeDialog.hint: Try Tab in each modal to experience the focus trap difference
nativeDialog.insight: Native <dialog> opens with a single showModal() call, but its focus trap is incomplete — JS supplementation is still required in production.

stacked.title: Stacked modals
stacked.description: Modals can be stacked on top of each other. Pressing ESC closes only the topmost one (LIFO).
stacked.hint: Open another modal from within a modal
stacked.insight: A module-level modal stack ensures ESC events only reach the topmost modal.
stacked.body1: You can open another modal on top of this one.
stacked.body2: Pressing ESC closes only this modal.
stacked.body3: This is the topmost modal. Try closing them one by one with ESC.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.description: Every interaction works with the keyboard alone.
keyboard.hint: Open the modal and operate it with keyboard only
keyboard.insight: Tab/Shift+Tab cycles focus, ESC closes immediately. Users without a mouse get an equal experience.
keyboard.body: Try Tab, Shift+Tab, and ESC to interact with the modal.

a11y.title: Accessibility (ARIA)
a11y.description: Follows the WAI-ARIA Dialog pattern. Screen reader users get the same experience.
a11y.insight: role="dialog" and aria-modal="true" must be part of the design from the start. aria-labelledby and aria-describedby let screen readers announce the modal's title and content.

attrs.modal: Communicates modal mode to screen readers
attrs.labelledby: Links to the Header title so screen readers announce it
attrs.describedby: Links to the Body content for additional description
